stories.headline: Gaza flotilla activists allege sexual assault and rape in Israeli detention
stories.summary: Israeli prison service denies claims of abuse during detention of 430 people trying to take aid to PalestiniansActivists released from Israeli custody after being detained on a flotilla trying to take aid to Gaza were subjected to abuse, organisers have alleged, with several hospitalised with injuries and at least 15 reporting sexual assaults, including rape.Israel’s prison service denied the allegations, and Reuters was not able to verify them i

stories.headline: Director of National Intelligence Chief Gabbard Is Stepping Down
stories.summary: Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard says she is resigning to help her husband fight a rare form of bone cancer. Her last day is expected to be on June 30. She's been increasingly out of step with the White House because of her anti-war views. Bloomberg's Jeff Mason reports.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Tulsi Gabbard to resign as US director of national intelligence
stories.summary: Gabbard who has been largely out of public view during recent US operations says she is leaving due to her husband's illness.

stories.headline: Global Long Bond Yields Hit Highest in Almost Two Decades
stories.summary: Subadra Rajappa, head of US rates strategy at Société Générale, joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Real Yield." The selloff in longer-maturity government bonds has pushed up yields to levels last seen during the global financial crisis, and market participants are warning the move has room to run. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Abrego Garcia Wins Dismissal of US Human-Smuggling Case
stories.summary: A federal judge in Tennessee dismissed human-smuggling charges against Salvadoran migrant Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia after concluding the US government brought the case in retaliation for his legal fight against being deported.

The slow-moving band of rainfall spanned 620 miles (1,000km) and travelled eastwards across the regions on Tuesday.
